basic_info.name: Design Educates Awards
basic_info.description: The Design Educates Awards recognize, showcase, and promote globally the best ideas and implementations of architecture and design that can educate.

Educates Awards 2026 for Meaningful Architecture and Design! The Design Educates Awards (DEAwards) recognize exceptional — Architecture — Design for Impact that are remarkable not only for their aesthetics, but also for their impact on society. Join the 8th edition of the Design Educates Awards 2026 for Meaningful Architecture and Design! The Design Educates Awards (DEAwards) recognize exceptional — Architecture — Design for Impact that are remarkable not only for their aesthetics, but also for their impact on society. What We Are Looking For What We Are Looking For We seek Architecture (1) that have the potential to make a meaningful contribution to the world – at every scale, from the smallest shelter or exhibition installation to residential, public, and urban-scale solutions. In addition to realized projects, conceptual proposals are also accepted and will be evaluated separately. The special category, Design for Impact (2), includes non-architectural designs, products, and concepts that create meaningful benefits for people, society, and the environment. We welcome participation from small to large firms, alongside emerging designers and established practices from around the world. Experience shows that among the winners, both groups are consistently represented. The submitted materials should preferably highlight the project's added value, articulating its significance beyond purely aesthetic qualities. Works must not be older than five years. Awards Brief Winners Gallery Outstanding International Jury Outstanding International Jury Our esteemed jury consists of world-renowned architects, designers, innovators and educators who are successfully expanding the boundaries of what is possible in their fields. Prof. Toyo Ito Architect, Founder of Toyo Ito & Associates, Laureate of the Pritzker Architecture Prize Edition 2026 Prof. Barbara Holzer Architect, Lecturer, Co-Founder and Director of Holzer Kobler Architekturen Edition 2026 Prof. Rainer Mahlamäki Partner & Co-Founder, Lahdelma & Mahlamäki architects Partner & Co-Founder, Lahdelma & Mahlamäki architects Edition 2026 Doriana Mandrelli Fuksas Co-Founder, Studio Fuksas, Architect and Designer leading "Fuksas Design“ Edition 2026 Prof. Carlo Ratti Architect, Founding Partner at CRA-Carlo Ratti Associati, Director at MIT Senseable City Lab Edition 2026 Prof. Thomas Schroepfer SUTD, Director Future Cities Laboratory Global Singapore-ETH Centre Edition 2026 Francine Houben Creative Director, Founding Partner at Mecanoo Edition 2026 David Basulto Founder of Archdaily Edition 2026 Elke Delugan-Meissl Founder and Partner at Delugan Meissl Associated Architects Founder and Partner at Delugan Meissl Associated Architects Edition 2025 Edition 2026 Dang Qun Co-Founder and Principal Partner of MAD Principal Partner of MAD Edition 2026 Edition 2026 Oskar Zięta Architect, Artist and Process Designer, Founder and CEO of Zieta Studio Edition 2026 Martin Reuter Managing Director of ingenhoven associates Edition 2026 Dr Peter Kuczia Architect, Founder of Kuczia Architects, Initiator and Curator of the Design Educates Awards Edition 2026 Check Jury members from previous editions here Check Jury members from previous editions here Architecture & Design with Added Value Architecture & Design with Added Value “Many architecture and design awards recognize and promote the “beauty” of successful design. But in the Design Educates Awards, we wanted to go one step further. We do not just want to see aesthetically and technically pleasing designs—we want something that offers true added value. We want an additional benefit with long-term implications that considers the increasing complexity of our lives, both now and in the future.“ Dr. Peter Kuczia Curator & Initiator of the DEAwards Competition Yearbook Competition Yearbook More about the idea of the awards in our post-competition books. 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024 2025 Dates Dates Submissions open: April 01, 2026 Project submission deadline: June 15,2026 Jury deliberation: July 2026 Announcement of winners: August 2026 Awards Ceremony in Warsaw, Poland 2026: October 16, 2026 Fees Fees Students: 50 EUR Individual professional / Company > 10 employees: 175 EUR Company 11 – 100 employees: 350 EUR Company 100+ employees: 650 EUR *If a project is submitted to both categories, the fee for the second category is reduced by 50% There are no further fees! The fees support the organization of the competition and the statutory activities of the non-profit Laka Foundation (host of the DEAwards). Pariticiaption in the awards ceremony is free. All winners receive printed and framed certificates and books for free at the ceremony. Winners Benefits Winners Benefits Winners Benefits In each category: (1) Architecture (2) Design for Impact following awards and recognition will be given: Winner of the Year Gold Prize Silver Prize Bronze Prize Special Recognition Honorable Mention In addition to the general evaluation, the student project with the highest score will be awarded the label: „Emerging Designer”. Additionally: 1) Winners packages with certificates and winning labels 2) Inclusion in the yearbook (ISBN registered) 3) Participation in the exhibition with confirmation by certificates 4) Complimentary attendance at the Award Ceremony in Warsaw, Poland, with photo documentation 5) Press kits 6) Permanent listing in the Winners Gallery on www.designeducates.com 7) Promotion through media sponsors and partners In each category: (1) Architecture (2) Design for Impact following awards and recognition will be given: Winner of the Year Gold Prize Silver Prize Bronze Prize Special Recognition Honorable Mention In addition to the general evaluation, the student project with the highest score will be awarded the label: „Emerging Designer”. Additionally: 1) Winners packages with certificates and winning labels 2) Inclusion in the yearbook (ISBN registered) 3) Participation in the exhibition with confirmation by certificates 4) Complimentary attendance at the Award Ceremony in Warsaw, Poland, with photo documentation 5) Press kits 6) Permanent listing in the Winners Gallery on www.designeducates.com 7) Promotion through media sponsors and partners 2019 Pismo Beach, USA 2022 2019 Asturias, Spain 2020 2019 Asturias, Spain 2021 2019 2019 Pismo Beach, USA 2022 2019 Asturias, Spain 2020 2019 Asturias, Spain 2021 What to Submit What to Submit The submission consists of the following files: 1. Images of your design saved as JPG files in RGB mode with 200 dpi (up to 5 files), including 2. A single featured image saved as JPG files in RGB mode with 200 dpi, in horizontal format, 2000 x 1000 px (this image will be used as a header image while presenting your design to the judges and media), 3. Description of your design (max. 1500 characters) and your team (max. 250 characters). All materials should be uploaded via the User Panel. Registration takes just 5 minutes! All materials should be uploaded via the User Panel.
